tasks.subject: marketing-faq-section [P1] Create FAQ data file with 7 Q&A items
tasks.description:
Create the FAQ data file at `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/lib/faq-items.ts`.

This file follows the same pattern as `subsystems.ts` — an exported interface and a const array of data objects. No JSX, no imports beyond types.

Create the file with this exact content:

```typescript
export interface FaqItem {
  id: string
  question: string
  answer: string
}

export const faqItems: FaqItem[] = [
  {
    id: 'what-is-agent',
    question: 'What do you mean by "agent"?',
    answer: 'An agent is an AI coding tool — like Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex — that can read, write, and run code on your machine. DorkOS doesn\'t replace your agents. It gives them the infrastructure to work when you\'re not watching: scheduling, communication, memory, and coordination.',
  },
  {
    id: 'how-different-from-claude-code',
    question: 'How is this different from just using Claude Code?',
    answer: 'Claude Code is the agent — the thing that thinks and writes code. DorkOS is the system around it. Without DorkOS, your agent stops when you close the terminal. With it, your agents run on schedules, message you when something breaks, coordinate with each other, and pick up where they left off.',
  },
  {
    id: 'data-privacy',
    question: 'Does DorkOS send any data to external servers?',
    answer: 'No. DorkOS runs entirely on your hardware. Session data stays in Claude Code\'s local transcript files. There are no accounts, no cloud dependency, and no telemetry phoning home.',
  },
  {
    id: 'getting-started',
    question: 'What do I need to get started?',
    answer: 'Node.js 18+ and Claude Code. One command installs DorkOS. No accounts to create, no cloud services to configure. If you can run `npm install`, you\'re ready.',
  },
  {
    id: 'license',
    question: 'What license is DorkOS under?',
    answer: 'MIT. Use it commercially, fork it, modify it, ship it. No restrictions.',
  },
  {
    id: 'remote-server',
    question: 'Can I run DorkOS on a remote server?',
    answer: 'Yes. DorkOS runs wherever you put it — your laptop, a VPS, a Raspberry Pi, a cloud VM. Built-in tunnel support lets you access it from anywhere.',
  },
  {
    id: 'cost',
    question: 'Is DorkOS free?',
    answer: 'DorkOS is free and open source. The agents themselves use API credits from their providers — running Claude Code overnight might cost a few dollars depending on the work. DorkOS doesn\'t add any cost on top of that.',
  },
]
```

Key details:
- The `id` field is used as the React key and the accordion `value` prop
- All 7 questions and answers are copied verbatim from the ideation document (Section 7)
- Single quotes for strings, escaped internal single quotes with `\'`
- No trailing comma issues — standard array formatting

Acceptance criteria:
- File exists at `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/lib/faq-items.ts`
- Exports `FaqItem` interface and `faqItems` const array
- Array contains exactly 7 items with unique `id` values
- All copy matches the ideation document verbatim
- TypeScript compiles without errors
tasks.activeForm: Creating FAQ data file with question and answer items

tasks.subject: marketing-faq-section [P1] Create FAQSection accordion component
tasks.description:
Create the FAQ section component at `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/ui/FAQSection.tsx`. This component follows the `SubsystemsSection.tsx` pattern: a `'use client'` directive, motion-wrapped container with STAGGER/REVEAL viewport animations, and content rendered from the companion data file. Create the file with this exact content: ```tsx 'use client' import { motion } from 'motion/react' import { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent, } from '@/components/ui/accordion' import { faqItems } from '../lib/faq-items' import { REVEAL, STAGGER, VIEWPORT } from '../lib/motion-variants' /** FAQ accordion section — handles residual objections before the install CTA. */ export function FAQSection() { return (
{/* Eyebrow */} Questions {/* Accordion */} {faqItems.map((item) => ( {item.question}

{item.answer}

))}
) } ``` Key design decisions: - `bg-cream-secondary` background alternates from `cream-white` (IdentityClose above) and `cream-primary` (InstallMoment below, via SubsystemsSection) - `max-w-2xl` matches the narrow content width used by other text-focused sections - Single `REVEAL` on the accordion container — the accordion items do NOT individually stagger (they are a cohesive block, not a list of cards) - `text-warm-gray leading-relaxed` on answer paragraphs matches the site's muted copy style - No custom accordion border overrides needed — the default shadcn `border-b last:border-b-0` dividers work with the warm palette since the border token is already warm-tinted in the site's Tailwind config - The Accordion component from `@/components/ui/accordion` uses Base UI (not Radix), with `keepMounted` by default and `data-[panel-open]` for chevron rotation - The component takes no props — fully self-contained like SubsystemsSection and HonestySection - Eyebrow uses the same pattern as SubsystemsSection: `font-mono text-2xs uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-brand-orange mb-6 block text-center` Acceptance criteria: - File exists at `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/ui/FAQSection.tsx` - Has `'use client'` directive at the top - Imports motion, accordion primitives, faqItems data, and motion variants - Uses STAGGER container with REVEAL children - Renders all 7 FAQ items via `.map()` with `item.id` as key - Uses `bg-cream-secondary` background - TypeScript compiles without errors

tasks.subject: marketing-faq-section [P1] Update barrel exports and page composition
tasks.description:
Two files need modification: the marketing barrel export and the homepage page component.

### 1. Update barrel exports: `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/index.ts`

Add three new exports. Insert them in the appropriate sections of the existing file:

In the "UI components — homepage" section (after the `IdentityClose` export, before the `// UI components — chrome` comment):
```typescript
export { FAQSection } from './ui/FAQSection'
```

In the "Data" section (after the `timelineEntries` export):
```typescript
export { faqItems } from './lib/faq-items'
```

In the "Types" section (after the `TimelineEntry` export):
```typescript
export type { FaqItem } from './lib/faq-items'
```

The full affected sections should look like:
```typescript
// UI components — homepage (new narrative arc)
export { Prelude } from './ui/Prelude'
export { ActivityFeedHero } from './ui/ActivityFeedHero'
export { VillainSection } from './ui/VillainSection'
export { PivotSection } from './ui/PivotSection'
export { TimelineSection } from './ui/TimelineSection'
export { SubsystemsSection } from './ui/SubsystemsSection'
export { HonestySection } from './ui/HonestySection'
export { InstallMoment } from './ui/InstallMoment'
export { IdentityClose } from './ui/IdentityClose'
export { FAQSection } from './ui/FAQSection'

// ... (chrome and legacy sections unchanged)

// Data
export { projects } from './lib/projects'
export { philosophyItems } from './lib/philosophy'
export { systemModules } from './lib/modules'
export { useCases } from './lib/use-cases'
export { villainCards } from './lib/villain-cards'
export { subsystems } from './lib/subsystems'
export { timelineEntries } from './lib/timeline-entries'
export { faqItems } from './lib/faq-items'

// ... (Motion section unchanged)

// Types
export type { Project, PhilosophyItem, NavLink } from './lib/types'
export type { SystemModule } from './lib/modules'
export type { UseCase } from './lib/use-cases'
export type { VillainCard } from './lib/villain-cards'
export type { Subsystem, Integration } from './lib/subsystems'
export type { TimelineEntry } from './lib/timeline-entries'
export type { FaqItem } from './lib/faq-items'
```

### 2. Update page composition: `apps/site/src/app/(marketing)/page.tsx`

Add `FAQSection` to the import statement from `@/layers/features/marketing`:
```typescript
import {
  Prelude,
  ActivityFeedHero,
  VillainSection,
  PivotSection,
  TimelineSection,
  SubsystemsSection,
  HonestySection,
  InstallMoment,
  IdentityClose,
  FAQSection,
  MarketingNav,
  MarketingHeader,
  MarketingFooter,
} from '@/layers/features/marketing'
```

Then add `<FAQSection />` between `<IdentityClose>` and `<InstallMoment>` in the JSX:
```tsx
<IdentityClose email={siteConfig.contactEmail} />

<FAQSection />

<InstallMoment />
```

`FAQSection` takes no props — it is fully self-contained.

Acceptance criteria:
- `index.ts` exports `FAQSection` component, `faqItems` data, and `FaqItem` type
- `page.tsx` imports `FAQSection` from the marketing barrel
- `FAQSection` is rendered between `IdentityClose` and `InstallMoment` in the page
- No existing exports or component order is changed
- TypeScript compiles without errors
tasks.activeForm: Updating barrel exports and page composition

tasks.subject: marketing-faq-section [P1] Verify site build succeeds
tasks.description:
Run the site build to validate all new and modified files compile correctly:

```bash
pnpm build --filter=@dorkos/site
```

This validates:
- TypeScript compilation passes with no type errors in `faq-items.ts` and `FAQSection.tsx`
- All imports resolve correctly (barrel exports, component imports, data imports, motion variant imports, accordion component imports)
- No build-time rendering errors from Next.js
- The `'use client'` directive is correctly placed
- Base UI accordion types are compatible with the usage pattern

If the build fails:
- Check for typos in import paths (especially `../lib/faq-items` and `../lib/motion-variants`)
- Verify the accordion import path `@/components/ui/accordion` resolves correctly
- Check that `bg-cream-secondary` is a valid Tailwind token in the site's config
- Ensure `text-2xs` is defined in the Tailwind config (it's a custom size used by other marketing sections)

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm build --filter=@dorkos/site` exits with code 0
- No TypeScript errors
- No build warnings related to the new files
